description: Add a new workspace crate implementing a clean chess library without bitboards, using an 8x8 matrix board representation and supporting legal move generation, move application, tests, and perft for comparison with chesslib.

userStories.title: Add non-bitboard crate to workspace
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a dedicated library crate in the workspace so that I can build and test the non-bitboard implementation independently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a new library crate to the workspace members in the root Cargo.toml
Choose a crate name that clearly distinguishes it from chesslib
The workspace builds successfully with cargo build --workspace
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created chesslib-simple crate with cargo new --lib, automatically added to workspace members.

userStories.title: Implement matrix-based board model
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the board represented as an 8x8 matrix without bitboards so that the implementation is easy to read and reason about.
acceptanceCriteria: Represent the board using an 8x8 matrix structure such as [[Option<Piece>; 8]; 8]
Model piece kind, color, side to move, castling rights, and en passant state explicitly
Do not use bitboards internally in the new crate
The crate builds successfully after introducing the board model
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented Color, PieceKind, Piece, CastlingRights, Square, and Board types in lib.rs. Board uses [[Option<Piece>; 8]; 8] matrix. No bitboards.

userStories.title: Implement move type and basic move application
userStories.description: As a developer, I want normal legal moves and captures to be represented and applied so that the library can produce child positions for search and perft.
acceptanceCriteria: Define a move type that can represent at least normal moves and captures
Applying a normal move updates board squares and side to move correctly
Applying a capture removes the captured piece from the destination square correctly
Include automated tests covering representative normal move and capture cases
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added Move struct with from/to squares. Board::apply_move returns new board with piece moved, capture handled implicitly, side_to_move toggled, halfmove clock and fullmove number updated.

userStories.title: Implement pawn pseudo-legal move generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pawns to generate forward moves, double pushes, and captures so that the board can enumerate basic pawn moves.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate pawn single pushes when the target square is empty
Generate pawn double pushes from the starting rank when both intermediate and target squares are empty
Generate pawn diagonal captures against opposing pieces
Include automated tests covering white and black pawn movement cases
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added pseudo_legal_pawn_moves() to Board. Single pushes, double pushes from start rank, diagonal captures. Updated apply_move to set en_passant_file on double pushes.

userStories.title: Implement knight and king pseudo-legal move generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want knights and kings to generate pseudo-legal moves so that the library can cover short-range piece movement.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate knight moves to all valid target squares not occupied by friendly pieces
Generate king moves to adjacent valid target squares not occupied by friendly pieces
Do not include castling in this story
Include automated tests covering representative knight and king movement cases
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added pseudo_legal_knight_moves() and pseudo_legal_king_moves(), both backed by a shared short_range_moves() helper. 9 new tests, 29 total passing.

userStories.title: Implement bishop, rook, and queen pseudo-legal move generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want sliding pieces to generate pseudo-legal moves so that the library can enumerate long-range piece movement without bitboards.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate bishop moves along diagonals until blocked
Generate rook moves along ranks and files until blocked
Generate queen moves as the union of bishop and rook movement
Stop sliding movement at the first blocking piece and allow capture of an opposing blocker only
Include automated tests covering representative bishop, rook, and queen movement cases
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Filter pseudo-legal moves into legal moves
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the library to reject moves that leave my king in check so that generated moves are legal chess moves.
acceptanceCriteria: Add legal move generation that filters out pseudo-legal moves leaving the moving side in check
Detect whether a king is currently attacked in a given board state
Exclude moves by pinned pieces when the move would expose the king illegally
Include automated tests covering check and pinned-piece scenarios
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement castling generation and application
userStories.description: As a developer, I want castling to be generated and applied correctly so that legal move generation covers this special king move.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate kingside and queenside castling only when castling rights, empty path, and legal attack conditions are satisfied
Applying a castling move relocates both king and rook correctly
Update castling rights correctly after king or rook movement
Include automated tests covering allowed and disallowed castling cases
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement en passant generation and application
userStories.description: As a developer, I want en passant to be generated and applied correctly so that legal move generation matches the full chess rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Track en passant availability after a legal pawn double push
Generate en passant captures only when the position allows them
Applying an en passant move removes the captured pawn from the correct square
Reject en passant moves that would leave the moving side in check
Include automated tests covering allowed and disallowed en passant cases
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement promotion generation and application
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pawn promotions to be generated and applied correctly so that move generation and perft cover promotion branches.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate promotion moves when a pawn reaches the last rank
Support promotion choices for queen, rook, bishop, and knight
Applying a promotion replaces the pawn with the selected promoted piece
Include automated tests covering promotion by quiet move and by capture
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add perft function
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a perft function that counts leaf nodes so that I can measure move generation correctness recursively.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose a perft function that counts leaf nodes for a requested depth
Depth 0 returns 1 for a valid board state
Depth 1 returns the number of generated legal moves
Include automated tests covering basic low-depth perft behavior
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add starting-position perft reference tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want perft tests for the standard starting position so that I can validate the implementation against canonical node counts.
acceptanceCriteria: Add perft tests for the standard starting position at agreed reference depths
Expected node counts match canonical starting-position perft values for the tested depths
The tests run through the new crate public API
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add special-case perft tests reused from chesslib
userStories.description: As a developer, I want special-case perft reference positions adapted from chesslib so that I can compare the non-bitboard implementation against the existing library on the same scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: Add additional perft tests adapted from the same core reference scenarios already used by chesslib
Include at least one reference position covering castling rights
Include at least one reference position covering en passant availability
Include at least one reference position covering promotion opportunities
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
